note: Running catalog / source of truth for the coin-archive site. Photos are the HEIC files in ~/Desktop/COINS (converted to JPG for viewing). lat/lng to be geocoded at build time. locationConfidence: confirmed | inferred | best-guess.

people.Jack: Grandfather; the collector. Signs cards 'J' or 'Jack'.
people.Greg: Jack's grandson; creator of this project.

coins.locationResolved: Walgreens, 2400 N Broadway St, Knoxville, TN 37917
coins.locationNote: True 1999 store unknown (Walgreens was just entering Knoxville ~2000); Greg chose 2400 N Broadway as a plausible for-fun pin.

coins.cardText: Legion Field J 10/15/83 Birmingham T-41 - A-34

event.name: Tennessee at Alabama football
event.detail: Upset of 14-point-favorite Alabama; Tennessee won late on Johnnie Jones' 66-yard touchdown run.

coins.familyMemory: Greg attended this game with Jack. (More of Greg's recollections to be added.)

coins.cardText: Jack - found UT Stadium 9/13/86 - UT/Miss St football game. Jack & Adam

event.name: Tennessee vs Mississippi State football
event.detail: Mississippi State upset No. 8 Tennessee at Neyland Stadium; attendance 89,897.

coins.familyMemory: Adam (Jack's grandson, Greg's brother) was at this game with Jack.

coins.locationResolved: White Stores, 5400 Clinton Hwy, Knoxville, TN
coins.locationNote: Defunct East-TN grocery chain. 5400 Clinton Hwy location documented in Knoxville city directories (via Groceteria.com) for 1960/1965/1970/1974/1980 — open in 1972. In Jack's own Inskip neighborhood (Clinton Hwy corridor, near his home off Paula Rd).

coins.locationResolved: Nixon's Deli North, 508 Merchants Dr, Knoxville, TN 37912
coins.locationNote: Nixon's Deli (local chain, founded 1976) had several Knoxville locations. Chose the 'North' store at 508 Merchants Dr — by far the closest to Jack's house off Paula Rd in Inskip (Merchants Dr is his neighborhood corridor, same area as the White Store on Clinton Hwy). Assumes that storefront existed in 1998 (plausible, not directly verified).

coins.cardText: March 10, 1985 - Lahaina, Maui - Pioneer Inn. J
coins.locationResolved: Pioneer Inn, 658 Wharf St, Lahaina, Maui, HI 96761
coins.locationNote: Historic 1901 hotel — oldest on Maui, longest continuously operating hotel in Hawaii; beside the famous Lahaina banyan tree on the harbor. Jack's farthest-flung find.

place.name: Pioneer Inn
place.where: Lahaina waterfront, beside the banyan tree, Maui, Hawaii (658 Wharf St)
place.story: The Pioneer Inn opened in 1901 on the Lahaina waterfront — the town's first hotel, and for more than a century the oldest on Maui and the longest continuously operating hotel in Hawaii. A two-story plantation-style building beside the great banyan tree, it kept watch over the harbor for 122 years and hosted everyone from sea captains to movie stars. When Jack found this dime out front in March 1985, the Inn had already stood for 84 years. On August 8, 2023, the wildfire that swept through Lahaina burned it to the ground. The coin outlasted the building.
place.photoCredit: Historic American Buildings Survey, Library of Congress (public domain)

coins.cardText: J. found - 11/2/91 UT/Mem. St game outside stadium

place.name: Neyland Stadium
place.where: Banks of the Tennessee River, University of Tennessee, Knoxville
place.story: Neyland Stadium has stood on the bank of the Tennessee River in Knoxville since 1921, growing from a few thousand seats into one of the largest stadiums in America — a sea of orange that packs in well over 100,000 on game day. Named for the legendary coach Gen. Robert Neyland, it has been the Saturday heartbeat of the city for a century. Jack found coins here more than once.

event.name: Tennessee vs Memphis State football
event.detail: Tennessee routed Memphis State at Neyland; attendance 96,664 (televised on TBS).

coins.cardText: 10/15/85 Jack found lot Super X Clinton Highway
coins.locationRaw: Super X parking lot, Clinton Highway
coins.locationResolved: SupeRx drugstore, Clinton Highway, Knoxville, TN (exact address not pinned)
coins.locationNote: Found in the parking lot ('found lot'). Exact Clinton Hwy address not pinned; placed on Clinton Hwy as a home-corridor best guess (same road as the White Store at 5400 Clinton Hwy). Pin-able via a 1985 city directory if ever wanted.

place.name: Super X (SupeRx)
place.where: Clinton Highway, North Knoxville (Jack's home corridor)
place.story: Super X — more properly SupeRx — was the Kroger company's drugstore chain, launched in 1961 and grown to hundreds of stores across the South and Midwest. When Jack found this penny in its Clinton Highway parking lot in October 1985, the chain was in its final stretch under Kroger: within a year Kroger sold the SupeRx stores off, and by 1994 the name was gone entirely, absorbed into Revco (later CVS). It was an ordinary neighborhood drugstore a short drive from Jack's house — and like the White Store down the road, it has vanished.

coins.cardNote: No finder initial written on this card; attributed to Jack (the collector) by default.
coins.locationResolved: Kmart #3065, Clinton Plaza Shopping Center, 5094 Clinton Hwy, Knoxville, TN 37912
coins.locationNote: Confirmed: Kmart #3065 at Clinton Plaza, 5094 Clinton Hwy — opened Nov 29, 1973 (replaced a smaller Kmart in the same plaza, so effectively the one Clinton Hwy Kmart), closed 2003. Memorial Day weekend find. A few blocks from the White Store (5400 Clinton Hwy) — tight home cluster.

place.name: Kmart (Clinton Plaza)
place.where: Clinton Plaza Shopping Center, 5094 Clinton Highway, North Knoxville
place.story: The Clinton Highway Kmart — store #3065 — anchored the Clinton Plaza Shopping Center at 5094 Clinton Highway. The big store Jack knew opened on November 29, 1973, replacing a smaller Kmart in the same plaza, and it ran for three decades before closing in 2003. For a North Knoxville family it was the everything-store: garden hoses, records, blue-light specials. Jack found this penny in its parking lot on the Saturday of Memorial Day weekend, 1986.

coins.cardText: 9/20/88 Jack found Las Vegas casino floor
coins.locationNote: Specific casino unknown (per Greg) — pin dropped in the middle of the Las Vegas Strip on purpose.

place.name: The Las Vegas Strip
place.story: Somewhere on the Las Vegas Strip, on a casino floor, Jack spotted a quarter on the carpet and pocketed it. There's no telling which casino, so this pin sits in the middle of the Strip. September 1988 caught Las Vegas at the tail end of its old self: the megaresort era was about to begin — The Mirage would open just over a year later, in November 1989, and remake the Strip for good. The Vegas Jack walked was still the older neon-and-lounge city of the era just before.
